content: We Work Remotely: Remote jobs in design, programming, marketing and more https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs.rss We Work Remotely: Remote jobs in design, programming, marketing and more en-US 60 Memora ApS: Founding Engineer Anywhere in the World Sjælland AWS, PostgreSQL, React, and TypeScript Full-Stack Programming Full-Time <img src="https://we-work-remotely.imgix.net/logos/0171/5449/logo.gif?ixlib=rails-4.0.0&w=50&h=50&dpr=2&fit=fill&auto=compress" /> <p> <strong>Headquarters:</strong> Copenhagen, Denmark <br /><strong>URL:</strong> <a href="https://memora.dk/">https://memora.dk/</a> </p> <h3>About Us</h3> <div> <p>At Memora, we're digitizing one of the oldest and most overlooked categories on earth: the cemetery. Most still run on paper maps, spreadsheets, and institutional memory. We are replacing this with an ecosystem for everyone who touches a cemetery: the leadership running it, the field teams maintaining it, the families visiting it, and the local partners serving them.&nbsp;</p> <p>We’re backed by leading investors and guided by a team of experienced founders and engineers from well known tech names reimagining a category long overdue change. We’re deliberately lean, and will keep it that way.</p> </div> <h3>Job Description</h3> <div> <p>This is a remote position.</p> <h1><span class="highlight"><span class="colour"><span class="font"><span class="size">Founding Engineer - Memora</span></span></span></span></h1> <p><span class="highlight"><span class="colour"><span class="font"><span class="size">We're hiring a Founding Engineer to step into one of the broadest roles on the team: A hands-on IC role with real product and cultural ownership.</span></span></span></span></p> <h2><span class="highlight"><span class="colour"><span class="font"><span class="size">The Why</span></span></span></span></h2> <p><span class="highlight"><span class="colour"><span class="font"><span class="size">Memora is growing rapidly. We need an awesome engineer in the team who can lead from all angles of the technical stack:</span></span></span></span></p> <ul> <li> <p><span class="highlight"><span class="colour"><span class="font"><span class="size">IaC end-to-end (Terraform, AWS, deploys, observability)</span></span></span></span></p> </li> <li> <p><span class="highlight"><span class="colour"><span class="font"><span class="size">Data ingestion pipelines (large exports, QGIS, PostGIS, multi tenant setups)</span></span></span></span></p> </li> <li> <p><span class="highlight"><span class="colour"><span class="font"><span class="size">Shipping core product features across NestJS, React, and React Native (Expo) mobile apps</span></span></span></span></p> </li> <li> <p><span class="highlight"><span class="colour"><span class="font"><span class="size">Being a thoughtful, methodical technical voice in a small team where every engineer has outsized influence</span></span></span></span></p> </li> </ul> <h2><span class="highlight"><span class="colour"><span class="font"><span class="size">The What (real examples from what we shipped in the last 30 days)</span></span></span></span></h2> <ul> <li> <p><span class="highlight"><span class="colour"><span class="font"><span class="size">Attribute-based filter systems across PostGIS, NestJS APIs, the React, and PMTiles-backed map tiles, so our clients can visualize by contracts, preservation status to even the flower season.</span></span></span></span></p> </li> </ul> <ul> <li> <p><span class="highlight"><span class="colour"><span class="font"><span class="size">Data import pipelines that convert raw PDF cemetery maps into geo-referenced GeoPackages in QGIS, then ingesting into PostGIS. Owning the end-to-end onboarding for new clientele.</span></span></span></span></p> </li> <li> <p><span class="highlight"><span class="colour"><span class="font"><span class="size">Terraform: multi-AZ RDS, ECS Fargate autoscaling, CI workflows, AI guardrails, WAF, SNS, and much more.</span></span></span></span></p> </li> <li> <p><span class="highlight"><span class="colour"><span class="font"><span class="size">AI: Designing, evaluating and building harnesses where prompts for summaries and activity can be elegantly delivered to communities, tuning guardrails.</span></span></span></span></p> </li> <li> <p><span class="highlight"><span class="colour"><span class="font"><span class="size">Production foundations such as RLS (multi-tenancy), idempotency keys + optimistic locking… so on</span></span></span></span></p> </li> </ul> <h2><span class="highlight"><span class="colour"><span class="font"><span class="size">The Stack</span></span></span></span></h2> <ul> <li> <p><span class="highlight"><span class="colour"><span class="font"><span class="size">Backend: NestJS, TypeScript, MikroORM, PostgreSQL + PostGIS, Inngest for durable workflows</span></span></span></span></p> </li> <li> <p><span class="highlight"><span class="colour"><span class="font"><span class="size">Web: React, TanStack Query, MapLibre + PMTiles</span></span></span></span></p> </li> <li> <p><span class="highlight"><span class="colour"><span class="font"><span class="size">Mobile: React Native + Expo, Expo Router, native iOS/Android push</span></span></span></span></p> </li> <li> <p><span class="highlight"><span class="colour"><span class="font"><span class="size">Infra: AWS (ECS Fargate, RDS, S3, CloudFront, SNS, Bedrock, WAF), Terraform, GH actions (OIDC), Logto</span></span></span></span></p> </li> <li> <p><span class="highlight"><span class="colour"><span class="font"><span class="size">Observability: Grafana Cloud, Sentry, CloudWatch</span></span></span></span></p> </li> <li> <p><span class="highlight"><span class="colour"><span class="font"><span class="size">Geospatial tooling: QGIS, GeoPackage, PMTiles</span></span></span></span></p> </li> <li> <p><span class="highlight"><span class="colour"><span class="font"><span class="size">AI: AWS Bedrock + guardrails, Promptfoo for evals</span></span></span></span></p> </li> </ul> <h2><span class="highlight"><span class="colour"><span class="font"><span class="size">Who you are</span></span></span></span></h2> <p><span
